category: stale meta description contradicting current page body

consistency.notes: All non-null, non-empty label values are supported by the page text. 'coffee.roaster_country' is entailed by 'Roasted in Portland, Oregon' together with the U.S. state/location reference. 'coffee.is_blend' is supported by the single-origin presentation and origin details for Ethiopia Bombe Natural rather than a multi-origin blend.

realism.reasons:
Plausible specialty coffee product page overall. The structure is very typical: breadcrumb, product title, price, bag size, stock/add-to-cart, concise specs (producer, station, origin, region, process, variety, altitude, harvest), tasting notes, a short sensory paragraph, a sourcing/process paragraph, brew recommendation, shipping note, and footer links. The voice reads like real modern roaster copy—specific but not overwrought—and the coffee details are coherent for a Sidama natural from Bombe, including varieties 74110/74112, raised-bed drying, and a light filter roast framing.

The variant block also looks believable: separate 250g and 2 lb options, SKUs in a plausible format, and grams matching the listed sizes closely enough.

Main tell lowering it from a 5: the meta description is for an entirely different coffee (Guatemala Los Volcanes), which is a realistic e-commerce mistake but still a noticeable inconsistency in this isolated text. A few details feel slightly too neatly assembled for synthetic catalog copy—for example the very polished sensory language and the explicit '[default_for_inference]' marker in the variant data, which would not normally appear in a raw html-to-text rendering. Still, this is close to real and only has minor tells.

label.coffee.sensory_text: Tasting notes: Strawberry jam, jasmine, milk chocolate. This season's Bombe Natural is all about ripe red fruit and florals without tipping into boozy territory. We roast it light for filter brewing to keep the cup lifted: strawberry jam up front, a jasmine-like aroma as it cools, and a rounded milk chocolate finish.
label.coffee.producer_text: This lot was assembled from Bombe smallholder farmers delivering ripe cherries to Bombe Washing Station in Bensa, Sidama. Cherries were floated, hand-sorted and dried slowly on raised beds for 18–22 days, with regular turning during the hottest parts of the day.

coffee.producer_text: This lot was assembled from Bombe smallholder farmers delivering ripe cherries to Bombe Washing Station in Bensa, Sidama. Cherries were floated, hand-sorted and dried slowly on raised beds for 18–22 days, with regular turning during the hottest parts of the day.
